# Executive Summary

## Synopsis

The executive summary presents the document's key findings on global energy
transition scenarios through 2030, covering demand shifts, supply buildout
requirements, and headline policy recommendations under three named scenarios.

## Portfolio Lens

From the energy-transition lens, this chapter foregrounds the acceleration of
renewable deployment and the structural decline of unabated coal. The net-zero
pathway requires tripling clean energy investment by 2030.

## Key numbers and projections

- Global renewable capacity reaches 8,500 GW by 2030 under stated policies
- Fossil fuel demand peaks in 2026 under the net-zero scenario
- Clean energy investment must reach $4.5 trillion per year by 2030

## Open questions

- Whether emerging economies can access sufficient financing at the required pace
- Feasibility of the assumed rate of efficiency improvement in heavy industry
